level_signals: 5+ years of consumer product management experience
Proven track record of measurable impact on acquisition, activation, or retention at scale
Direct ownership over key growth metrics

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of consumer product management experience
Proven ownership of growth, activation, retention, and/or monetization metrics
Proven track record of measurable impact on acquisition, activation, or retention at scale
Strong analytical skills (cohort analysis, funnels, experimentation frameworks)
Experience designing, running, and interpreting A/B tests
Ability to move fast, prioritize ruthlessly, and operate in ambiguity
Strong design sensibility and UX intuition

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong intuition for consumer behavior, psychology, and entertainment-driven products
High taste level with respect for creative product DNA
Background in social, gaming, entertainment, or subscription consumer products
Comfortable with scrappy experimentation and rapid iteration
Able to balance monetization goals with long-term user trust and delight

scope.surface: Activation funnels, onboarding flows, growth loops, retention mechanics, and monetization optimization across mobile and web
scope.business_model: B2C consumer AI platform, freemium/subscription model with entertainment, social, and gaming characteristics
core_responsibilities: Own activation funnels, onboarding flows, and new user experience optimization
Design and execute A/B experiments across activation, retention, and monetization
Build and iterate on growth loops and viral mechanics
Partner with data science on cohort analysis, funnel metrics, and experimentation rigor
Collaborate with core product to ensure new features are instrumented for growth impact
Partner with monetization on driving subscription conversion and optimizing the freemium model
Identify friction points across the user lifecycle using quantitative and qualitative insights
Define, track, and report on key growth metrics (activation rate, D1/D7/D30 retention, LTV)

green_flags: Clear ownership of specific, measurable growth metrics (activation rate, D1/D7/D30, LTV)
Well-defined product surface and scope—not vague
Platform at meaningful scale (20M+ MAU, unicorn status) offering genuine growth challenges
Company has demonstrated product-market fit and recognition (Google Play AI App of the Year)
Cross-functional collaboration model is clearly articulated
red_flags: No location information provided—remote/hybrid/onsite status entirely unclear
JD language around 'Ideal Candidate Profile' and 'What They're Looking For' is not clearly delineated from hard requirements, creating ambiguity about must-have vs. nice-to-have boundaries

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no management responsibility mentioned)
ambiguities: The 'What They're Looking For' and 'Ideal Candidate Profile' sections are not labeled as 'preferred' or 'nice to have' but describe aspirational traits rather than hard requirements; classified as nice_to_haves based on framing and contrast with the 'Key Requirements' section.
No location or work arrangement is specified anywhere in the JD; location.type set to 'unclear'.
No visa sponsorship or work authorization language present; set to 'unclear'.
